steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence sets up a contrast: the data were previously inaccessible, but a transfer project made them something. The blank must name the opposite of inaccessible — a word meaning usable or reachable.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'relevant': relevance is about applicability, not access. A project that transfers data makes it reachable, not necessarily more relevant. ✗
  • Choice B — 'notable': means worthy of attention. That does not logically follow from solving an access problem. ✗
  • Choice C — 'available': means accessible or obtainable — the direct opposite of inaccessible and the logical result of a successful data transfer project. ✓
  • Choice D — 'readable': too narrow; it implies a formatting issue rather than the broader access problem the passage describes. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
